IMPORTANT: Our C-Mod dataset is NOT comparable to DisruptionBench

why.our_dataset: MIT PSFC Open Density Limit DB — contains ONLY density-limit disruptions
why.disruptionbench: Full C-Mod disruption DB — ALL types: density limit, locked mode, impurity injection, VDE
why.implication: Density-limit disruptions are TRIVIALLY separable (mean density AUC=0.90). Our GBT AUC=1.0 on this easy subset says NOTHING about matching CCNN on the hard full set.

what_ccnn_0.974_means: Tested on ALL disruption types including fast impurity injection (millisecond precursors) and locked modes. This is MUCH harder than density-limit-only.
to_fairly_compare: Need full C-Mod disruption database from MDSplus (alcdata.psfc.mit.edu). Contact disruption-py@mit.edu.
honest_verdict: We cannot claim to beat or match CCNN because we're solving a fundamentally easier problem.
